contributor: university of north carolina at chapel hill library, chapel hill, nc
description: Friday November 16 1634 Bailer in Dispute about Charge That Columbia Is Being Used as Medium for Fascist Propaganda Nicholas Murray Butler president of Columbia University and the No ten have been engaged in a dispute over the Notions charge that the Italian department of Columbia is used as chief source and center of Fascist propaganda in America The article in which the charge was made is characterized by President Butler in a letter to the Nation as a curious hodgepodge of falsehood misrepresentation and halftruth with the fesult that it ends in non sense The Nation replying to his letter says that it is clear that Dr Butler has been grossly deceived by subordinates whom he has apparently trusted and that his letter With its numerous irrelevahcies fails to an swer or even to mention many of the specific points made ifi the artile on Fascism He is asked to go behind and returns and check the facts for himself The Casa Italiana a handsome sev enstory building is tbd home of the Italian department of Columbia and is an integral part of the Univer sity and yet says the Nation it has become an unofficial adjunct of the
